feats.title: Lost Faith
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Apostate ----'''Special: Hidden'''
Lost Faith is a Hidden Feat. It can only be given to you by the Game Master and you cannot talk about this Feat with other Players. [[Category:Hidden]] ----'''Special'''
This Feat brings no advantage. It could be a permanent condition of your character or a temporary one. By default it is [[Lost Faith (temporary)]] and will disappear at the next scenario. But if it is specified that it is permanent [[Lost Faith (permanent)]] you would need a [[Miracle]] to cure it. [[Faith]] can always be used to prevent your character from receiving this condition. ---- '''Description'''
Your character is mentally broken, has has lost faith in humanity and god. He may repress it and act like a zealot, but deep in his soul lies the truth: He have renounced his faith. The stars are aligned against you. Every time you need to roll a die 6, roll a second die. If the result is 1 on the second die, take this for the result of the roll. Also, you are much more susceptible to mental pressure and will have a violent reaction (cowing, sobbing, running, screaming or brute force) in case of mental duress. You cannot use the Feat [[Courage]], [[Faith]] or [[Instinct]] as well as, at the Game Master's discretion, any Prowess in relation with god. If your background justifies it and if you Game Master agrees, you can worship a dark god, in which case you gain the [[⛧ Dark Faith]] Feat. [[Category:Official]] [[Category:Feats]]
feats.raw_content:
{{Feats
| Synonyms = Apostate
| Prowess? = No
| Archetype? = No
| Official? = Yes
| Hidden? = Yes
| Special = 
This Feat brings no advantage. It could be a permanent condition of your character or a temporary one.
By default it is [[{{PAGENAME}} (temporary)]] and will disappear at the next scenario.
But if it is specified that it is permanent [[{{PAGENAME}} (permanent)]] you would need a [[Miracle]] to cure it.
[[Faith]] can always be used to prevent your character from receiving this condition.
| Description = 

Your character is mentally broken, has has lost faith in humanity and god.

He may repress it and act like a zealot, but deep in his soul lies the truth: He have renounced his faith.

The stars are aligned against you. Every time you need to roll a die 6, roll a second die. If the result is 1 on the second die, take this for the result of the roll.

Also, you are much more susceptible to mental pressure and will have a violent reaction (cowing, sobbing, running, screaming or brute force) in case of mental duress.

You cannot use the Feat [[Courage]], [[Faith]] or [[Instinct]] as well as, at the Game Master's discretion, any Prowess in relation with god.

If your background justifies it and if you Game Master agrees, you can worship a dark god, in which case you gain the [[⛧ Dark Faith]] Feat.

}}

feats.title: Luck
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Lucky ----'''Special: Prowess'''
Luck is a [[:Category:Prowesses|Prowess]], this special kind of [[:Category:Feats|Feat]] is an action that can only be done once per scenario. To acquire it, you need to already have another Feat related to it, for example : No prerequisites, anyone can take this Prowess. Your Game Master may accept to waive such prerequisites if your background or in game actions justify it. ---- '''Description'''
Your character possesses extraordinary luck, often escaping situations unscathed where survival seemed impossible. You may use this Prowess to reroll any dice. Alternatively, you can use this Prowess to roll a D6: *On a roll of 1, 2, 3, or 4, this Prowess has no effect, but it still counts as being used. *On a roll of 5 or 6, the character temporarily gains one of the following Feats: [[Faith]], [[Daring Escape]], [[Reflexes]], [[Marksmanship]] or [[Well Prepared]]. [[Category:Official]] [[Category:Prowesses]] [[Category:Feats]]

feats.title: Manipulative
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Seductive ---- '''Description'''
The character can detect what motivates people and take advantage of it, he is a great manipulator. After a short conversation, he understands the deep desires of his interlocutors and knows how to take advantage of them. He knows how to influence weak minds and make them indebted to him. This is rarely enough to turn an enemy against his own side. This Feat is not a drawback. Thus the character is not necessarily a compulsive manipulator or a perverse narcissistic manipulator psychopath. He is simply able to manipulate others if the situation requires it. [[Category:Official]] [[Category:General Feats]] [[Category:Feats]]

feats.title: Marksmanship
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Hawk's Eye, Bull's Eye, Trueshot, Good Thrower, Throw Aim ---- '''Description'''
The character never missed a target. When chosing this feat, he has to chose a speciality : ranged or throwing Mark this feat as [[Marksmanship (ranged)]] or [[Marksmanship (throwing)]] on your sheet depending on your choice. =====Ranged===== Players with this feat can take aim and hit their mark perfectly with a weapon designed for ranged shooting, such as a bow, a sling shot, or a crossbow (Note that a crossbow is only allowed for shooting pagans(non Christians), and is thusly forbidden among civilians and rare among non crusade/templar forces.) This feat also counts toward the use of ranged siege weapons and fortress defence weapons such as trebuchets and ballistas, should the scenario allow for their usage. Unlike Marksmanship(throwing), this feat requires preparing a weapon, specific ammo, and taking aim, and thus cannot be used as an emergency action unless the character is already engaged in combat, or has prepared for combat. =====Throwing===== Players with this feat can throw any reasonably throwable object they carry in their inventory, and it will hit the spot they aim for perfectly, provided their targets are also in reasonable range. (E.g. within 20 meters, same as a baseball pitch) Unlike Marksmanship(ranged), this feat requires minimal preparation, and therefore can be used as an emergency reaction. [[Category:Official]] [[Category:General Feats]] [[Category:Feats]]
feats.raw_content:
{{Feats
| Synonyms = Hawk's Eye, Bull's Eye, Trueshot, Good Thrower, Throw Aim
| Prowess? = No
| Archetype? = No
| Official? = Yes
| Description = 

The character never missed a target.

When chosing this feat, he has to chose a speciality : ranged or throwing

Mark this feat as [[Marksmanship (ranged)]] or [[Marksmanship (throwing)]] on your sheet depending on your choice.

=====Ranged=====

Players with this feat can take aim and hit their mark perfectly with a weapon designed for ranged shooting, such as a bow, a sling shot, or a crossbow 
(Note that a crossbow is only allowed for shooting pagans(non Christians), and is thusly forbidden among civilians and rare among non crusade/templar forces.)

This feat also counts toward the use of ranged siege weapons and fortress defence weapons such as trebuchets and ballistas, should the scenario allow for their usage.

Unlike Marksmanship(throwing), this feat requires preparing a weapon, specific ammo, and taking aim, and thus cannot be used as an emergency action unless the character is already engaged in combat, or has prepared for combat.

=====Throwing=====

Players with this feat can throw any reasonably throwable object they carry in their inventory, and it will hit the spot they aim for perfectly, provided their targets are also in reasonable range. (E.g. within 20 meters, same as a baseball pitch)

Unlike Marksmanship(ranged), this feat requires minimal preparation, and therefore can be used as an emergency reaction.

}}

A Prowess can only be used once per scenario and allows the character to do a special action.

A Prowess is a type of non-visual spell. It is possible only if there is an explanation to the limit of the scientifically plausible and it is up to the player to justify it with the game master before using his prowess.

For example, it is humanly impossible to heal someone who has been decapitated, but it is not impossible to treat someone who's throat have been slit. It is very difficult, very rare, but scientifically plausible.

Like any Feats, a character can only take a Prowess once. 

-->

feats.title: Monk
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Benedictine, Order of Saint Benedict, Franciscan, Order of Friars Minor, Dominican, Order of Preachers, Carthusians, Order of Saint Bruno, Chartreux ----'''Special: Archetype'''
Monk is an [[:Category:Archetypes|Archetype]], it is a special kind of [[:Category:Feats|Feat]]. A character can only have one Archetype in total. Apart from that, Archetypes are like normal Feats and may be taken at character creation or on any level up. On your sheet, mark Monk as one of your Feats and add all Feats gained separately. ---- '''Description'''
Monasteries in the middle age, are a very rare opportunity for those seeking to change their social status. With minor exceptions, people lived and died in the same craft as their parents. Monasteries were a rare occasion for people to chose their destiny. Most monastic orders are derived from the Order of Saint Benedict and follow a core set of rules. All monks have to make the vows of obedience, poverty and chastity, to pray at regular intervals within the day and to spend most of their life in dedication to god and their order. Each order is known for different reasons. Benedictines are the most numerous monks by far. They are organised in large monasteries and are renown for their love of books and calligraphy. Most well known philosophers, alchemists and scholars were from this order. Carthusians are also organised in monasteries, but they are known for their austerity, where members lived in their own cells and spent several hours a day in prayer and meditation. They are also famous for harsh means of self imposed repentance using flagellation and wearing painful cilices as often as necessary. Franciscans and Dominicans are mendicant orders, known for their lifestyle of poverty, traveling, and living in urban areas for purposes of preaching, evangelization, and ministry, especially to the poor. Dominicans are also known because most inquisitors where chosen from their ranks. If you character is a Dominican [[Inquisitor]], a Benedictine [[Alchemist]], a Carthusian [[Ecclesiastic (abbot)|Abbot]] or a [[Knight Hospitaller]], it is better to take those feats instead. A [[Monk (benedictine)]] gains the Feat [[Knowledge]]. A [[Monk (franciscan)]] gains the Feat [[Survival]]. A [[Monk (dominican)]] gains [[Survival]] or a Feat of his choice in the list of Feats granted by the [[Inquisitor]] archetype. A [[Monk (carthusians)]] gains the Feat [[Fanatic]] or [[Confession]]. A [[Monk (other)]] gains the Feat [[Knowledge]]. If his background justifies it, he also gains the [[⭐ Faith]] Feat (regardless of his order). Additionally, he gains one Feat of his choice in this list : *[[Knowledge]] *[[Fanatic]] *[[Confession]] *[[Sleepless]] *[[Knowledge (apothecary)]] *[[Knowledge (medicine)]] *[[Knowledge (cryptography)]] *[[Noble]] *[[Photographic Memory]] *[[Reputation]] *[[Well Connected]] *[[Miracle]] (even if the character doesn't meet the prerequisites) *[[Revelation]] (even if the character doesn't meet the prerequisites) *[[Talented]] *[[Foreigner]] *[[Background]] *[[Handicap (venerable age)]] Unless his background justifies it, the character cannot take the [[Combat]], [[Strong]] or [[True Strike]] Feats. Note that not all monks have this feat and the character may de facto be a monk from any of those orders without having it. [[Category:Official]] [[Category:Archetypes]] [[Category:Feats]]
feats.raw_content:
{{Feats
| Synonyms = Benedictine, Order of Saint Benedict, Franciscan, Order of Friars Minor, Dominican, Order of Preachers, Carthusians, Order of Saint Bruno, Chartreux
| Prowess? = No
| Archetype? = Yes
| Official? = Yes
| Description = 

Monasteries in the middle age, are a very rare opportunity for those seeking to change their social status.

With minor exceptions, people lived and died in the same craft as their parents. Monasteries were a rare occasion for people to chose their destiny.

Most monastic orders are derived from the Order of Saint Benedict and follow a core set of rules.

All monks have to make the vows of obedience, poverty and chastity, to pray at regular intervals within the day and to spend most of their life in dedication to god and their order.

Each order is known for different reasons.

Benedictines are the most numerous monks by far. They are organised in large monasteries and are renown for their love of books and calligraphy. Most well known philosophers, alchemists and scholars were from this order.

Carthusians are also organised in monasteries, but they are known for their austerity, where members lived in their own cells and spent several hours a day in prayer and meditation. They are also famous for harsh means of self imposed repentance using flagellation and wearing painful cilices as often as necessary.

Franciscans and Dominicans are mendicant orders, known for their lifestyle of poverty, traveling, and living in urban areas for purposes of preaching, evangelization, and ministry, especially to the poor.

Dominicans are also known because most inquisitors where chosen from their ranks.

If you character is a Dominican [[Inquisitor]], a Benedictine [[Alchemist]], a Carthusian [[Ecclesiastic (abbot)|Abbot]] or a [[Knight Hospitaller]], it is better to take those feats instead.


A [[Monk (benedictine)]] gains the Feat [[Knowledge]].

A [[Monk (franciscan)]] gains the Feat [[Survival]].

A [[Monk (dominican)]] gains [[Survival]] or a Feat of his choice in the list of Feats granted by the [[Inquisitor]] archetype.

A [[Monk (carthusians)]] gains the Feat [[Fanatic]] or [[Confession]].

A [[Monk (other)]] gains the Feat [[Knowledge]].


If his background justifies it, he also gains the [[⭐ Faith]] Feat (regardless of his order).

Additionally, he gains one Feat of his choice in this list :
*[[Knowledge]]
*[[Fanatic]]
*[[Confession]]
*[[Sleepless]]
*[[Knowledge (apothecary)]]
*[[Knowledge (medicine)]]
*[[Knowledge (cryptography)]]
*[[Noble]]
*[[Photographic Memory]]
*[[Reputation]]
*[[Well Connected]]
*[[Miracle]] (even if the character doesn't meet the prerequisites)
*[[Revelation]] (even if the character doesn't meet the prerequisites)
*[[Talented]]
*[[Foreigner]]
*[[Background]]
*[[Handicap (venerable age)]]

Unless his background justifies it, the character cannot take the [[Combat]], [[Strong]] or [[True Strike]] Feats.

Note that not all monks have this feat and the character may de facto be a monk from any of those orders without having it.

}}

To do that, please create an account and select "Watch this page" while editing this Feat. 

Advices on creating Feats :

A Feat is a massive advantage granted to the finest of the finest after a life of training. Most normal people don't have Feats at all.
A person with the Agile Feat, is qualified to be in the Olympics (not win, but at least participate)
A person with the Rich Feat is the richest man in his city.
Etc..

While creating a Feat, make sure with your Game Master that there is no other Feat that would be Synonym (e.g. would have the same final game effect). If yes, add it as a Synonym rather than create a new Feat.

For example, Courage is a synonym of Fanatism, not because they are the same concept, but because they have a similar in game effect.

And finally, make sure that the Feat is useful but not too useful, this depends on the scenarios and campaign you're playing.

Advices on creating Archetypes :

A typical archetype is either :
One Feat imposed and two to be chosen in a list
or
A disadvantage that grants two feats freely chosen.

The goal of an Archetype is to guide a new player on how to create a very cool and coherent character.


Advices on creating Prowesses :

A Prowess can only be used once per scenario and allows the character to do a special action.

A Prowess is a type of non-visual spell. It is possible only if there is an explanation to the limit of the scientifically plausible and it is up to the player to justify it with the game master before using his prowess.

For example, it is humanly impossible to heal someone who has been decapitated, but it is not impossible to treat someone who's throat have been slit. It is very difficult, very rare, but scientifically plausible.

Unlike normal Feats, a character can acquire the same Prowess several times. 

-->

feats.title: Necromancy
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Incubation, Dream Incubation ----'''Special: Prowess'''
Necromancy is a [[:Category:Prowesses|Prowess]], this special kind of [[:Category:Feats|Feat]] is an action that can only be done once per scenario. To acquire it, you need to already have another Feat related to it, for example : [[Knowledge (heresy)]] or [[Instinct]] Your Game Master may accept to waive such prerequisites if your background or in game actions justify it. ----'''Special: Hidden'''
Necromancy is a Hidden Feat. It can only be given to you by the Game Master and you cannot talk about this Feat with other Players. [[Category:Hidden]] ---- '''Description'''
After a long ritual consisting of breathing powerful incenses and spending hours searching and reading about the person, the character will fall asleep on the grave or next to the corpse of a decreased person. Dream Incubation is the earliest form of Necromancy, it allows the character to enter in contact with a deceased person in his dreams. He can have a conversation with him, live episodes of his past life or [[Disguise]] as him for a day. The corpse should have no missing limbs but there is no limit as to how far in time the person died. Note that the character may or may not recall all details accurately when he wakes up. [[Category:Official]][[Category:Dark Feats]] [[Category:Prowesses]] [[Category:Feats]]

| Prowess? = Yes
| Prowess Prerequisites = [[Knowledge (heresy)]] or [[Instinct]]
| Hidden? = Yes

| Official? = Yes
| Archetype? = No
| Description = 

After a long ritual consisting of breathing powerful incenses and spending hours searching and reading about the person, the character will fall asleep on the grave or next to the corpse of a decreased person.

Dream Incubation is the earliest form of Necromancy, it allows the character to enter in contact with a deceased person in his dreams.

He can have a conversation with him, live episodes of his past life or [[Disguise]] as him for a day.

The corpse should have no missing limbs but there is no limit as to how far in time the person died.

Note that the character may or may not recall all details accurately when he wakes up.

}}

feats.title: Noble
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Baron, Baronet, Duke, Count, Viscount ---- '''Description'''
The character is Noble from a particularly prestigious lineage. This status offers him both a Big Network, he is Rich and has a certain Reputation. He owns an estate and one additional servant follows him at all times. The prestige of his house grants him the right to kill any commoner who displeased him without much more justification. To other members of the nobility he has the [[Reputation]] Feat permanently, for others a roll is required. If the character is attempting an action in relation with those qualities, he must roll a die six. With no possibility to roll again for this whole situation. On a 1,2,3,4 this Feat has no effect. On a 5 or 6 the character is considered to have one of the following feats of his choice : [[Well Connected]], [[Rich]] or [[Reputation]] In case the dice roll failed, the character in not in a position to make a big expense at the moment, his reputation didn't reach his interlocutor or he is momentarily out of favours with his network. This quality is not a drawback, so he is not necessarily weak. Note that some characters can be de facto noble without having this quality, their lineage simply isn't well known. [[Category:Official]] [[Category:General Feats]] [[Category:Feats]]
feats.raw_content:
{{Feats
| Synonyms = Baron, Baronet, Duke, Count, Viscount
| Prowess? = No
| Official? = Yes
| Special = Leave blank if none.
| Description = 

The character is Noble from a particularly prestigious lineage. This status offers him both a Big Network, he is Rich and has a certain Reputation.

He owns an estate and one additional servant follows him at all times.

The prestige of his house grants him the right to kill any commoner who displeased him without much more justification.

To other members of the nobility he has the [[Reputation]] Feat permanently, for others a roll is required.

If the character is attempting an action in relation with those qualities, he must roll a die six. With no possibility to roll again for this whole situation.

On a 1,2,3,4 this Feat has no effect.
On a 5 or 6 the character is considered to have one of the following feats of his choice : [[Well Connected]], [[Rich]] or [[Reputation]]

In case the dice roll failed, the character in not in a position to make a big expense at the moment, his reputation didn't reach his interlocutor or he is momentarily out of favours with his network.

This quality is not a drawback, so he is not necessarily weak.

Note that some characters can be de facto noble without having this quality, their lineage simply isn't well known.

}}

feats.title: Outlaw Wisdom
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Thief Guild, Bandit Connections ---- '''Description'''
The character is particularly knowledgable about outlaws, thieves, bandits, beggars and sell-swords. He can locate and contact any local thief guild, bandit gang and the likes. He will know how to purchase anything illegal (drugs, poisons etc...) and outlaws identify him as one of their own. For example, he could contact the local thief guild and make sure a certain person is robbed of a particular item or that a particular lock is picked. The time he requires to do this depends on the size of the town he is currently in. Given enough times, he can also hire a cut-throat that will kill a person he designates. He may also enquire those outlaws on certain topics, such as local rumours. [[Category:Official]] [[Category:General Feats]] [[Category:Feats]]

feats.title: Photographic Memory
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Total memory, Eidetic memory, Hypermnesic memory ---- '''Description'''
The character is able to remember images and sounds in every detail. He is able to invoke in his mind all his memories and scrutinize those scenes in every detail. For example, he is able to memorize a book by simply looking at each page. He can also phonetically repeat phrases he has heard in a language he does not understand. He perceives images and sounds like a photograph and he can discover afterwards details that has escaped him. This Feat does not imply an [[Eye for Detail]] [[Category:Official]] [[Category:General Feats]] [[Category:Feats]]
feats.raw_content:
{{Feats

}}

feats.title: Physiognomist
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' First sight ---- '''Description'''
The character can analyse a person at first sight The character knows how to collect information by briefly looking at a person, he has a great capacity for analysis. He does not detect lies particularly well, but will know if a person is disturbed, tired, uncomfortable, brutal, cruel, too sure of herself or others. Among other things, it allows to determine the Feats another character has (if any) The common man has no Feats at all. The character must actively analyse the person in question and his diagnosis is not infallible. In particular, the character can not unmask a character having the [[Trickster]] Feat [[Category:Official]] [[Category:General Feats]] [[Category:Feats]]

}}

feats.title: Pickpocket
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Cutpurse, Thief, Steal, Illusionist ----'''Special: Prowess'''
Pickpocket is a [[:Category:Prowesses|Prowess]], this special kind of [[:Category:Feats|Feat]] is an action that can only be done once per scenario. To acquire it, you need to already have another Feat related to it, for example : [[Stealth]], [[Outlaw Wisdom]], [[Reflexes]], [[Lock Picking]] or [[Trickster]] Your Game Master may accept to waive such prerequisites if your background or in game actions justify it. ---- '''Description'''
Your character have mastered the art of diverting attention. Like a master illusionist, he can steal or place any items of his choosing on his oblivious victim. Your character inspects another unsuspecting person and instantly knows each and every items he wears or carries. You can take any items of your choice, replace them with items you own of plant anything on the victim. The other character will notice the missing item within a reasonable amount of time, few seconds if someone stole his sword, to few hours if someone stole a letter in his bag. The most daring pickpocket operations are possible, even stealing socks, bracelets or any item attached to the person. The only exception being if the target is very defensive, hostile or knows that he is being targeted. [[Category:Official]] [[Category:Prowesses]] [[Category:Feats]]

| Archetype? = No

feats.title: Poison
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Curse, Toxin, Venom ----'''Special: Prowess'''
Poison is a [[:Category:Prowesses|Prowess]], this special kind of [[:Category:Feats|Feat]] is an action that can only be done once per scenario. To acquire it, you need to already have another Feat related to it, for example : [[Knowledge (apothecary)]] or [[Outlaw Wisdom]] Your Game Master may accept to waive such prerequisites if your background or in game actions justify it. ---- '''Description'''
You buy or craft a perfect poison. It may be a breathing, contact of ingestion poison, it is perfectly impossible to detect. You can only craft one dose to affect one normal person. This person will feel the effect instantaneously or after an chosen number of minutes, hours or days. This poison may kill, put to sleep or sicken your target. The effect can seem natural or not. [[Category:Official]] [[Category:Prowesses]] [[Category:Feats]]

A disadvantage that grants two feats freely chosen.

The goal of an Archetype is to guide a new player on how to create a very cool and coherent character.


Advices on creating Prowesses :

A Prowess can only be used once per scenario and allows the character to do a special action.

A Prowess is a type of non-visual spell. It is possible only if there is an explanation to the limit of the scientifically plausible and it is up to the player to justify it with the game master before using his prowess.

For example, it is humanly impossible to heal someone who has been decapitated, but it is not impossible to treat someone who's throat have been slit. It is very difficult, very rare, but scientifically plausible.

Unlike normal Feats, a character can acquire the same Prowess several times. 

-->

feats.title: Polyglot
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Linguist, Multilingual ---- '''Description'''
In most situations, the players are either talking to a learned men, in which case they speak in Latin or they are in a region speaking one of the main languages and hence can communicate. But in remote corners of Europe, such as Ireland, Isle of Man, the Sultanate of Grenada, the Pagan far East, etc, the local illiterate populations only speak a local language. In which case the players can hire a translator, but cannot communicate directly with locals, hence being unable to use any communication based skills such as Detect Lies. Using this Feat you are miraculously able to speak, read and write almost any languages and can use any communication based Feats directly on any illiterate local. [[Category:Official]] [[Category:General Feats]] [[Category:Feats]]

| Prowess? = No

| Archetype? = No

In which case the players can hire a translator, but cannot communicate directly with locals, hence being unable to use any communication based skills such as Detect Lies.

Using this Feat you are miraculously able to speak, read and write almost any languages and can use any communication based Feats directly on any illiterate local.

}}

Advices on creating Feats :

A Feat is a massive advantage granted to the finest of the finest after a life of training. Most normal people don't have Feats at all.
A person with the Agile Feat, is qualified to be in the Olympics (not win, but at least participate)
A person with the Rich Feat is the richest man in his city.
Etc..

While creating a Feat, make sure with your Game Master that there is no other Feat that would be Synonym (e.g. would have the same final game effect). If yes, add it as a Synonym rather than create a new Feat.

For example, Courage is a synonym of Fanatism, not because they are the same concept, but because they have a similar in game effect.

And finally, make sure that the Feat is useful but not too useful, this depends on the scenarios and campaign you're playing.

Advices on creating Archetypes :

A typical archetype is either :
One Feat imposed and one to be chosen in a list
or
A disadvantage that grants two feats freely chosen plus one Miracle.

The goal of an Archetype is to guide a new player on how to create a very cool and coherent character.


Advices on creating Prowesses :

A Prowess can only be used once per scenario and allows the character to do a special action.

A Prowess is a type of non-visual spell. It is possible only if there is an explanation to the limit of the scientifically plausible and it is up to the player to justify it with the game master before using his prowess.

For example, it is humanly impossible to heal someone who has been decapitated, but it is not impossible to treat someone who's throat have been slit. It is very difficult, very rare, but scientifically plausible.

Unlike normal Feats, a character can acquire the same Prowess several times. 

-->

feats.title: Puppet Master
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Hypnosis ----'''Special: Prowess'''
Puppet Master is a [[:Category:Prowesses|Prowess]], this special kind of [[:Category:Feats|Feat]] is an action that can only be done once per scenario. To acquire it, you need to already have another Feat related to it, for example : [[Manipulative]], [[Trickster]], [[Charismatic]], [[Knowledge (apothecary)]] Your Game Master may accept to waive such prerequisites if your background or in game actions justify it. ---- '''Description'''
You hypnotise up to five people for hours and implant a program deep into their minds. The program is triggered each time a very specific condition is met, for example : "Each time you hear a certain word" "When you feel you've been discovered by the inquisition" "Once, one year from now" etc... The program is any action you want the person to make. It cannot be something the person is very unwilling to do. For example, you can't force someone to disclose something he doesn't want to disclose or you can't force someone to suicide. But you can force someone to drink a "healing potion" which is in fact a very potent poison. Examples of programs and triggers : "If you are tortured by the inquisition, loose all ability to speak / scream incoherently" "Each time you see the leader of this criminal organisation, attack relentlessly everyone around you" "One year from now, drink this potion" etc... Depending on the willpower of the person, he may resists the hypnosis (with the Feat Courage in particular) or he may need several sessions. The program stays implanted in the mind of the person for a certain amount of time, it could be weeks, months, years or a lifetime, depending on a lot of factors. [[Category:Official]] [[Category:Prowesses]] [[Category:Feats]]
feats.raw_content:
{{Feats
| Synonyms = Hypnosis
| Prowess? = Yes
| Prowess Prerequisites = [[Manipulative]], [[Trickster]], [[Charismatic]], [[Knowledge (apothecary)]]
| Official? = Yes
| Archetype? = No
| Description = 

You hypnotise up to five people for hours and implant a program deep into their minds.

The program is triggered each time a very specific condition is met, for example :

"Each time you hear a certain word"

"When you feel you've been discovered by the inquisition"

"Once, one year from now"

etc...

The program is any action you want the person to make. It cannot be something the person is very unwilling to do.

For example, you can't force someone to disclose something he doesn't want to disclose or you can't force someone to suicide. But you can force someone to drink a "healing potion" which is in fact a very potent poison. 

Examples of programs and triggers :

"If you are tortured by the inquisition, loose all ability to speak / scream incoherently"

"Each time you see the leader of this criminal organisation, attack relentlessly everyone around you"

"One year from now, drink this potion"

etc...

Depending on the willpower of the person, he may resists the hypnosis (with the Feat Courage in particular) or he may need several sessions.

The program stays implanted in the mind of the person for a certain amount of time, it could be weeks, months, years or a lifetime, depending on a lot of factors.


}}

feats.title: Read Thoughts
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Hear Thoughts, Mental Scrying ----'''Special: Prowess'''
Read Thoughts is a [[:Category:Prowesses|Prowess]], this special kind of [[:Category:Feats|Feat]] is an action that can only be done once per scenario. To acquire it, you need to already have another Feat related to it, for example : [[Detect Lies]], [[Instinct]], [[Interrogate]], [[Manipulative]] or [[Physiognomist]] Your Game Master may accept to waive such prerequisites if your background or in game actions justify it. ---- '''Description'''
While speaking with a character, you momentarily gain insights into his thoughts. Your character isn't hearing his thoughts per say, but using his deep psychological understanding, he manages to grasp everything his interlocutor is thinking. A [[Trickster]] will know he is being scrutinised and will chose carefully his thoughts. [[Category:Official]] [[Category:Prowesses]] [[Category:Feats]]
feats.raw_content:
{{Feats
| Synonyms = Hear Thoughts, Mental Scrying
| Prowess? = Yes
| Prowess Prerequisites = [[Detect Lies]], [[Instinct]], [[Interrogate]], [[Manipulative]] or [[Physiognomist]]
| Archetype? = No
| Official? = Yes

Your character isn't hearing his thoughts per say, but using his deep psychological understanding, he manages to grasp everything his interlocutor is thinking.

A [[Trickster]] will know he is being scrutinised and will chose carefully his thoughts.

}}

feats.title: Reflexes
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Dodge ---- '''Description'''
The character is constantly attentive and ready to react in the blink of an eye. At any moment, the character can decide to react at full speed within the limit of what is humanely possible. He is able to avoid an arrow or a thrown weapon, even to catch an arrow shot at him, he can catch any falling object within reach. It is almost impossible to hit him with a melee weapon. He is not particularly good at fighting. If the character is in a situation where [[Agile|Agility]] is required. He may roll a die 6. On a 1,2,3,4 this Feat has no effect. On a 5 or 6 the character is considered to have the [[Agile]] Feat momentarily. [[Category:Official]] [[Category:General Feats]] [[Category:Feats]]
feats.raw_content:
{{Feats
| Synonyms = Dodge
| Prowess? = No
| Official? = Yes
| Special = Leave blank if none.
| Description = 

The character is constantly attentive and ready to react in the blink of an eye.

At any moment, the character can decide to react at full speed within the limit of what is humanely possible.

He is able to avoid an arrow or a thrown weapon, even to catch an arrow shot at him, he can catch any falling object within reach.

It is almost impossible to hit him with a melee weapon.

He is not particularly good at fighting.

If the character is in a situation where [[Agile|Agility]] is required. He may roll a die 6.

On a 1,2,3,4 this Feat has no effect.

On a 5 or 6 the character is considered to have the [[Agile]] Feat momentarily.

}}

feats.title: Relic
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Artefact, Heirloom ---- '''Description'''
The church entrusted you (or your ancestors) with the protection of a sacred relic. It may be some bones, blood or a lock of hair from a saint. It may also be some splinters of a famous artefact, like the holy cross or the coat of saint Bernard. Your relic is encased in some way and you carry it with you at all times. For example in the tilt of your sword, around your neck or inside a book. As long as you are in the possession of it, you gain one free General Feat or Prowess of your choice, even if you don't meet the prerequisites or if this Feat is forbidden by your Archetype. The Feat chosen must have a relation with the relic and the player has to justify it. For example, a relic of Saint Antony the Great could give you the [[Heal]] Feat A relic of Saint George encased in a spear could give you the [[Combat (duel)]] Feat etc... If accepted by the Game Master, you may even have access to a [[:Category:Dark Feats|Dark Feat]]. [[Category:Official]] [[Category:General Feats]] [[Category:Feats]]
feats.raw_content:
{{Feats
| Synonyms = Artefact, Heirloom
| Prowess? = No
| Archetype? = No
| Official? = Yes
| Description = 

The church entrusted you (or your ancestors) with the protection of a sacred relic.

It may be some bones, blood or a lock of hair from a saint.
It may also be some splinters of a famous artefact, like the holy cross or the coat of saint Bernard.

Your relic is encased in some way and you carry it with you at all times. For example in the tilt of your sword, around your neck or inside a book.

As long as you are in the possession of it, you gain one free General Feat or Prowess of your choice, even if you don't meet the prerequisites or if this Feat is forbidden by your Archetype.

The Feat chosen must have a relation with the relic and the player has to justify it.

For example, a relic of Saint Antony the Great could give you the [[Heal]] Feat

A relic of Saint George encased in a spear could give you the [[Combat (duel)]] Feat etc...

If accepted by the Game Master, you may even have access to a [[:Category:Dark Feats|Dark Feat]].

}}

feats.title: Repented Heretic
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Luciferian, Witch, Albigensian, Waldensian, Dulcinian, Excommunicated ----'''Special: Archetype'''
Repented Heretic is an [[:Category:Archetypes|Archetype]], it is a special kind of [[:Category:Feats|Feat]]. A character can only have one Archetype in total. Apart from that, Archetypes are like normal Feats and may be taken at character creation or on any level up. On your sheet, mark Repented Heretic as one of your Feats and add all Feats gained separately. ---- '''Description'''
You have been given a choice. Either repent or burn. You chose the former. A common misconception is that the inquisition's task was to burn heretics, in fact the inquisition consistently spared any heretics who agreed to confess and repent, only those who refused to repent (or even worse, who retracted) had to be burned to purify their souls. Burning at the stake was an act of mercy because fire was believed to purify the soul and send heretics to heaven. Your character has believed or was forced to follow a heretic cult. Most heresies were started by catholic priests or monks who had a particular vision of the faith, that was later on ruled as heretic by the church. (the entire Franciscan order for example was ruled as a heresy before being absolved shortly thereafter) But the darkest heresies are secret organisations designed to trick the weak into serving the legions of hell. Some of your brethren decided to live their faith until the end and you saw them burn, the others like you where spared. Perhaps you even lead the inquisition to discover the cult when you realised it's true purpose. Alternatively, you may have committed a severe crime against god and the church excommunicated you. You have decided to make penance. Your repentance may or may not be sincere, depending if you took the Feat [[Lost Faith]]. Either way, the inquisition have mandated you to purge your sins by helping them for a given period of time. Part of your sentence is to use your knowledge about heresies to turn it against them. As a Repented Heretic, the character gains the Feat [[Knowledge (heresy)]]. If his background justifies it, he also gains the [[⭐ Faith]] Feat. (or potentially [[⛧ Dark Faith]] if he has [[Handicap (lost faith)]]) Additionally, he gains one Feat of his choice in this list : *[[Outlaw Wisdom]] *[[Trickster]] *[[Fanatic]] *[[Charismatic]] (you were a heretic leader) *[[Stealth]] *[[Reputation]] *[[Well Connected]] (some former members of your cult are high ranked) *[[Survival]] *[[Manipulative]] *[[Foreigner]] *[[Knowledge (cryptography)]] *[[Background]] *[[Magic Tricks]] *[[Eternal Youth]] (a pact?) (even if the character doesn't meet the prerequisites) *[[Curse]] (witchcraft ?) (even if the character doesn't meet the prerequisites) *[[Witchcraft]] (even if the character doesn't meet the prerequisites) *[[Necromancy]] (even if the character doesn't meet the prerequisites) *[[Handicap (lost faith)]] (your soul is still tainted by heresy) *[[Handicap (insane)]] (the things you saw... the things you did !) Unless his background justifies it, the character cannot take the [[Combat]], [[Strong]] or [[True Strike]] Feats. Note that not all repented heretics have this feat and the character may de facto have been a member of a cult without having it. [[Category:Official]] [[Category:Archetypes]] [[Category:Feats]]
feats.raw_content:
{{Feats
| Synonyms = Luciferian, Witch, Albigensian, Waldensian, Dulcinian, Excommunicated
| Prowess? = No
| Archetype? = Yes
| Official? = Yes
| Description = 

You have been given a choice. Either repent or burn. You chose the former.

A common misconception is that the inquisition's task was to burn heretics, in fact the inquisition consistently spared any heretics who agreed to confess and repent, only those who refused to repent (or even worse, who retracted) had to be burned to purify their souls. Burning at the stake was an act of mercy because fire was believed to purify the soul and send heretics to heaven.

Your character has believed or was forced to follow a heretic cult. Most heresies were started by catholic priests or monks who had a particular vision of the faith, that was later on ruled as heretic by the church. (the entire Franciscan order for example was ruled as a heresy before being absolved shortly thereafter)
But the darkest heresies are secret organisations designed to trick the weak into serving the legions of hell.

Some of your brethren decided to live their faith until the end and you saw them burn, the others like you where spared.
Perhaps you even lead the inquisition to discover the cult when you realised it's true purpose.

Alternatively, you may have committed a severe crime against god and the church excommunicated you. You have decided to make penance.

Your repentance may or may not be sincere, depending if you took the Feat [[Lost Faith]].
Either way, the inquisition have mandated you to purge your sins by helping them for a given period of time. Part of your sentence is to use your knowledge about heresies to turn it against them.


As a Repented Heretic, the character gains the Feat [[Knowledge (heresy)]].

If his background justifies it, he also gains the [[⭐ Faith]] Feat. (or potentially [[⛧ Dark Faith]] if he has [[Handicap (lost faith)]])

Additionally, he gains one Feat of his choice in this list :
*[[Outlaw Wisdom]]
*[[Trickster]]
*[[Fanatic]]
*[[Charismatic]] (you were a heretic leader)
*[[Stealth]] 
*[[Reputation]]
*[[Well Connected]] (some former members of your cult are high ranked)
*[[Survival]]
*[[Manipulative]]
*[[Foreigner]]
*[[Knowledge (cryptography)]]
*[[Background]]
*[[Magic Tricks]]
*[[Eternal Youth]] (a pact?) (even if the character doesn't meet the prerequisites)
*[[Curse]] (witchcraft ?) (even if the character doesn't meet the prerequisites)
*[[Witchcraft]] (even if the character doesn't meet the prerequisites)
*[[Necromancy]] (even if the character doesn't meet the prerequisites)
*[[Handicap (lost faith)]] (your soul is still tainted by heresy)
*[[Handicap (insane)]] (the things you saw... the things you did !)

Unless his background justifies it, the character cannot take the [[Combat]], [[Strong]] or [[True Strike]] Feats.

Note that not all repented heretics have this feat and the character may de facto have been a member of a cult without having it.

}}

feats.title: Reputation
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Famous, Prestigious ---- '''Description'''
For good or ill, the character or the institution he represents is known to all. Nobody can deny having heard of him at least in the region. Songs have been written about him and maybe even whole books. His reputation can lead other characters to help him in his quest, to attract his good favors or to cooperate with him out of fear. He is accompanied by an additional loyal henchman. He probably knows how to read and write a language. He may be of noble birth. [[Category:Official]] [[Category:General Feats]] [[Category:Feats]]

To acquire it, you need to already have another Feat related to it, for example : [[⭐ Faith]] or [[Instinct]] Your Game Master may accept to waive such prerequisites if your background or in game actions justify it. ---- '''Description'''
You gain some insight into the future. This insight might come from a dream, the usage of drugs or some form of divination. The Game Master can choose to give you some insights into the events to come or at least have to describe you in detail the next event on the Ambiance Track. feats.title: Rich
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Merchant, Bourgeois ---- '''Description'''
The character has incredible wealth. He probably has one or more domains and he can easily get anything that money can buy. He always moves with a large amount of money on him as well as letter of credits. Once he has access to a large city, he can make a withdrawal of the sum of his choice at the nearest Hospitaller counter. He is accompanied by an additional loyal henchman. He may be of noble birth. Once per scenario he can make a very substancial spending, like paying the taxes for a whole village. [[Category:Official]] [[Category:General Feats]] [[Category:Feats]]

For example, Courage is a synonym of Fanatism, not because they are the same concept, but because they have a similar in game effect.

And finally, make sure that the Feat is useful but not too useful, this depends on the scenarios and campaign you're playing.

Advices on creating Archetypes :

A typical archetype is either :
One Feat imposed and two to be chosen in a list
or
A disadvantage that grants two feats freely chosen.

The goal of an Archetype is to guide a new player on how to create a very cool and coherent character.


Advices on creating Prowesses :

A Prowess can only be used once per scenario and allows the character to do a special action.

A Prowess is a type of non-visual spell. It is possible only if there is an explanation to the limit of the scientifically plausible and it is up to the player to justify it with the game master before using his prowess.

For example, it is humanly impossible to heal someone who has been decapitated, but it is not impossible to treat someone who's throat have been slit. It is very difficult, very rare, but scientifically plausible.

Unlike normal Feats, a character can acquire the same Prowess several times. 

-->

feats.title: Scoundrel
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Assassin, Rogue, Thief, Bandit, Cut-throat ----'''Special: Archetype'''
Scoundrel is an [[:Category:Archetypes|Archetype]], it is a special kind of [[:Category:Feats|Feat]]. A character can only have one Archetype in total. Apart from that, Archetypes are like normal Feats and may be taken at character creation or on any level up. On your sheet, mark Scoundrel as one of your Feats and add all Feats gained separately. ---- '''Description'''
The character was recruited by the Inquisition as a way to avoid a stronger sentence. He lived his life as a Thief, a Bandit or any other kind of illegal activities. He gains the Feat [[Stealth]]. If his background justifies it, he also gains the [[⭐ Faith]] Feat. Additionally, he gains one Feat of his choice in this list : *[[Survival]] *[[Manipulative]] *[[Agile]] *[[Combat| Combat (duel or skirmish) ]] *[[Trickster]] *[[Lock Picking]] *[[Instinct]] *[[Reflexes]] *[[Outlaw Wisdom]] *[[Tracker]] (even if the character doesn't meet the prerequisites) *[[Guards !]] (thugs from your gang) (even if the character doesn't meet the prerequisites) *[[Background]] *[[Handicap (lost faith)]] Unless his background justifies it, the character cannot take any [[Knowledge]] Feats. [[Category:Official]] [[Category:Archetypes]] [[Category:Feats]]
feats.raw_content:
{{Feats
| Synonyms = Assassin, Rogue, Thief, Bandit, Cut-throat
| Prowess? = No
| Archetype? = Yes
| Official? = Yes
| Description = 
The character was recruited by the Inquisition as a way to avoid a stronger sentence.

He lived his life as a Thief, a Bandit or any other kind of illegal activities.

He gains the Feat [[Stealth]].

If his background justifies it, he also gains the [[⭐ Faith]] Feat.

Additionally, he gains one Feat of his choice in this list :
*[[Survival]]
*[[Manipulative]]
*[[Agile]]
*[[Combat| Combat (duel or skirmish) ]]
*[[Trickster]]
*[[Lock Picking]]
*[[Instinct]]
*[[Reflexes]]
*[[Outlaw Wisdom]]
*[[Tracker]] (even if the character doesn't meet the prerequisites)
*[[Guards !]] (thugs from your gang) (even if the character doesn't meet the prerequisites)
*[[Background]]
*[[Handicap (lost faith)]]

Unless his background justifies it, the character cannot take any [[Knowledge]] Feats.

}}

feats.title: Sleeper Agent
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Mind Control ----'''Special: Prowess'''
Sleeper Agent is a [[:Category:Prowesses|Prowess]], this special kind of [[:Category:Feats|Feat]] is an action that can only be done once per scenario. To acquire it, you need to already have another Feat related to it, for example : [[Spy Network]] and [[Acquaintance]] or [[Puppet Master]] Your Game Master may accept to waive such prerequisites if your background or in game actions justify it. ---- '''Description'''
After years of meticulous planning and manipulation, you carefully positioned and conditioned a Sleeper Agent to act upon your command. By using covert tactics such as deception, blackmail, and psychological manipulation, you have embedded this agent within a key position in society, ready to execute your plans when activated. Using this Prowess, you can reveal that one character of your choice was actually a sleeper agent at your command. The sleeper agent is controlled by the player and can provide critical assistance, carry out specific tasks, or gather information from within their unique position. Unlique an [[Acquaintance]], the Sleeper Agent is willing to die for the player. The Game Master may rule that a person is special and can't be influenced by this Prowess, he has to warn you before using the Feat. [[Category:Official]] [[Category:Prowesses]] [[Category:Feats]]
feats.raw_content:
{{Feats

| Prowess? = Yes

| Official? = Yes

By using covert tactics such as deception, blackmail, and psychological manipulation, you have embedded this agent within a key position in society, ready to execute your plans when activated.

Using this Prowess, you can reveal that one character of your choice was actually a sleeper agent at your command. The sleeper agent is controlled by the player and can provide critical assistance, carry out specific tasks, or gather information from within their unique position.

Unlique an [[Acquaintance]], the Sleeper Agent is willing to die for the player.

The Game Master may rule that a person is special and can't be influenced by this Prowess, he has to warn you before using the Feat.

}}

feats.title: Sleepless
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Insomnia, Never Sleep, Tireless, Endurance ---- '''Description'''
The character requires little to no sleep. He can stay awake for an entire week and still function properly. Under normal circumstances, he barely needs to sleep four hours per night. He is particularly endurant and can sustain physical effort five times longer than an average person. Medieval life often require short nights of sleep. The most devout monks will pray every two hours including at night and the most reliable messengers will walk and ride for days without sleep. A strong tolerance to sleep depravation can be vital in the most critical situations. [[Category:Official]] [[Category:General Feats]] [[Category:Feats]]

feats.title: Spy Network
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' King of Beggars, Cabinet noir, Intelligencers ----'''Special: Prowess'''
Spy Network is a [[:Category:Prowesses|Prowess]], this special kind of [[:Category:Feats|Feat]] is an action that can only be done once per scenario. To acquire it, you need to already have another Feat related to it, for example : [[Trickster]], [[Well Connected]], [[Manipulative]] or [[Outlaw Wisdom]] Your Game Master may accept to waive such prerequisites if your background or in game actions justify it. ---- '''Description'''
After years of preparations, the character have built a vast network of spies. From corrupt valets to beggars and prostitutes, using gold, blackmail and murder he have infiltrated most layers of society. Using this Prowess, the player receives a detailed report from his spy networks on a particular topic. The report always contain valuable and accurate informations, even though the report could be limited in his findings depending on the secrecy of the matter. The spy network isn't composed of particularly knowledgeable scholars, so it can emulate the [[Knowledge]] Feat without problem. But a 5 or a 6 on a die 6 roll is needed to emulate any detailed knowledge feat like [[Knowledge (heresy)]] or [[Outlaw Wisdom]]. [[Category:Official]] [[Category:Prowesses]] [[Category:Feats]]

feats.title: Stealth
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Hide, Discretion, Hide in a crowd ---- '''Description'''
The character knows how to disappear. He knows not to be noticed in a crowd and how to be forgotten by his past interlocutors and also to hide during a night infiltration. He also knows basics of disguise, so he can for example try to infiltrate a domain posing as a servant or a guard. His ability to lie in itself is not very convincing, but he knows how to avoid conversations that could unmask him. He can also, within the limit of humanly possible, navigate stealthily in a building without anyone noticing him. [[Category:Official]] [[Category:General Feats]] [[Category:Feats]]
feats.raw_content:
{{Feats
| Synonyms = Hide, Discretion, Hide in a crowd
| Prowess? = No
| Official? = Yes
| Special = Leave blank if none.
| Description = 

The character knows how to disappear.

He knows not to be noticed in a crowd and how to be forgotten by his past interlocutors and also to hide during a night infiltration.

He also knows basics of disguise, so he can for example try to infiltrate a domain posing as a servant or a guard. His ability to lie in itself is not very convincing, but he knows how to avoid conversations that could unmask him.

He can also, within the limit of humanly possible, navigate stealthily in a building without anyone noticing him.

}}

feats.title: Strong
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Athletic, Fighter, Muscular, Warrior, Barbarian, Brute, Soldier ---- '''Description'''
The character is inhumanely strong and vigorous. He can carry much heavier weights and bash most normal doors and chests. He is able to break any normal restraints. He cannot bash iron gates or heavy doors. You can swim. If the character is attempting an action in relation with those qualities, he must roll a die 6. With no possibility to roll again for this whole situation. On a 1, 2, 3, 4 this Feat has no effect. On a 5 or 6 the character is considered to have one of the following feats of his choice : [[Tough]], [[Combat (duel)]], [[Combat (skirmish)]] or [[Combat (monster)]] This Feat is not a drawback, it does not imply that the character is stupid or bullish. [[Category:Official]] [[Category:General Feats]] [[Category:Feats]]

{{Feats

}}

feats.title: Surprise Attack !
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Charge !, In the name of God ! ----'''Special: Prowess'''
Surprise Attack ! is a [[:Category:Prowesses|Prowess]], this special kind of [[:Category:Feats|Feat]] is an action that can only be done once per scenario. To acquire it, you need to already have another Feat related to it, for example : [[Charismatic]], [[Combat (battle)]], [[Favour]], [[Deus Vult !]] or [[Riot]] Your Game Master may accept to waive such prerequisites if your background or in game actions justify it. ---- '''Description'''
You call forth an army of 30 people to the rescue. As soon as your order is pronounced, arrows and catapulted rocks start falling from the sky and armed soldiers followed by cavaliers start flooding from every directions. Nobody expected it, nobody saw you organise it, but your troops where positioned this whole time waiting for the order ! Those soldiers might be your own army, soldiers loaned by the church or requested from a local lord. If the game master agrees, the attack happen immediately as if you have been preparing it for a long time. If you are in a situation that makes this surprise attack impossible (in a remote place, far away for the nearest army or without means of communications) the game master may allow you to do this attack in a few days time or not at all. The attack lasts for a short period of time, after which the soldiers will have to retreat. With your army's support, you are momentarily considered having the [[Combat (skirmish)]] or [[Combat (battle)]] Feats. [[Category:Official]] [[Category:Prowesses]] [[Category:Feats]]

| Archetype? = No
| Official? = Yes
| Description = 

You call forth an army of 30 people to the rescue.

As soon as your order is pronounced, arrows and catapulted rocks start falling from the sky and armed soldiers followed by cavaliers start flooding from every directions.

Those soldiers might be your own army, soldiers loaned by the church or requested from a local lord.

If the game master agrees, the attack happen immediately as if you have been preparing it for a long time.

If you are in a situation that makes this surprise attack impossible (in a remote place, far away for the nearest army or without means of communications) the game master may allow you to do this attack in a few days time or not at all.

The attack lasts for a short period of time, after which the soldiers will have to retreat.

With your army's support, you are momentarily considered having the [[Combat (skirmish)]] or [[Combat (battle)]] Feats.

}}

feats.title: Survival
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Man of the woods, Craft ---- '''Description'''
The character knows the basics of all the skills necessary for survival in the wild. Hunting, gathering, first aid, orientation etc ... The character can survive perpetually in autarky in a temperate climate with at most ten persons under his care. In an arid or Nordic climate, with a little preparation and as long as it is humanly possible, he can live a month. He is good at climbing mountains and have great sense of orientation. He knows very well which plant is edible and which plant isn't. The character is also able to craft almost anything quickly and with little to no tools and components. He can craft any simple object as long as he has access to raw materials and rudimentary tools. He can, for example make a barricade, an armour, a raft, a siege weapon, a tent, a trap etc ... The objects will be fully functional although rudimentary of appearance. The character can also easily perform repairs, such as filling a waterway in a boat, making a solid stretcher with two branches and cloth or replacing the wheel of a cart. This quality does not allow him to build an object that he does not understand, for example he can not manufacture a key perfectly adapted to a given lock. Unless he has a key model available. [[Category:Official]] [[Category:General Feats]] [[Category:Feats]]

The character is also able to craft almost anything quickly and with little to no tools and components.

The character can also easily perform repairs, such as filling a waterway in a boat, making a solid stretcher with two branches and cloth or replacing the wheel of a cart.

This quality does not allow him to build an object that he does not understand, for example he can not manufacture a key perfectly adapted to a given lock. Unless he has a key model available.

}}

feats.title: Talented
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Singing, Painting, Instruments, Creative Writing, Calligraphy, Cooking, Woodcraft, Smithing, Knowledge (theology), Knowledge (history), Knowledge (engineering), Knowledge (architecture), Knowledge (law) ---- '''Description'''
Your character is extremely talented in a chosen field that is unlikely to have a practical use in the game. His talent is instantly recognisable and anyone will be stunned at his mastery. He may have reached some amount of fame in his field and if your character also has the [[Reputation]] Feat you are actually very well known in all Christendom. See the Synonyms for examples of talents, but feel free to create your own and add it to the list. Unlike other Feats, this feat can be taken several times with a different designated talent. If your talent have a relation with a specific knowledge, it counts as a « Knowledge (something) » Feat. All characters with a specialised « Knowledge (something) » Feat are very literate. They are fluent in latin, greek and have at least some bases of a foreign language of their choice. They can read and write any languages they know. If the character is attempting an action related to the general [[Knowledge]] Feat, he must roll a die six. With no possibility to roll again for this whole situation. On a 1,2,3,4 this Feat has no effect. On a 5 or 6 the character is considered to have the [[Knowledge]] Feat momentarily. [[Category:Official]] [[Category:General Feats]] [[Category:Feats]]

While creating a Feat, make sure with your Game Master that there is no other Feat that would be Synonym (e.g. would have the same final game effect). If yes, add it as a Synonym rather than create a new Feat.

For example, Courage is a synonym of Fanatism, not because they are the same concept, but because they have a similar in game effect.

And finally, make sure that the Feat is useful but not too useful, this depends on the scenarios and campaign you're playing.

Advices on creating Archetypes :

A typical archetype is either :
One Feat imposed and two to be chosen in a list
or
A disadvantage that grants two feats freely chosen.

The goal of an Archetype is to guide a new player on how to create a very cool and coherent character.


Advices on creating Prowesses :

A Prowess can only be used once per scenario and allows the character to do a special action.

A Prowess is a type of non-visual spell. It is possible only if there is an explanation to the limit of the scientifically plausible and it is up to the player to justify it with the game master before using his prowess.

For example, it is humanly impossible to heal someone who has been decapitated, but it is not impossible to treat someone who's throat have been slit. It is very difficult, very rare, but scientifically plausible.

Unlike normal Feats, a character can acquire the same Prowess several times. 

-->

feats.title: Tough
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Diehard, Will to survive, Thick skin ---- '''Description'''
The character is almost impossible to kill. He has survived blows that would be deadly to anyone but him. In the limit of the humanly possible (decapitation, drowning, open artery etc ...) the character can not be killed by a physical injury. The most extreme damage will give him a lasting handicap at worst. Depending on the blows received, however, it is possible that he faint or needs a long period of recovery. In case of exposure to disease or poison, roll a die six. On a 1,2,3,4 this Feat has no effect. On a 5 or 6 the character is considered to have the [[Immune (diseases)]] or [[Immune (poisons)]] Feat momentarily. [[Category:Official]] [[Category:General Feats]] [[Category:Feats]]

{{Feats

| Description =

Depending on the blows received, however, it is possible that he faint or needs a long period of recovery.

In case of exposure to disease or poison, roll a die six.

On a 1,2,3,4 this Feat has no effect.
On a 5 or 6 the character is considered to have the [[Immune (diseases)]] or [[Immune (poisons)]] Feat momentarily.

}}

feats.title: Tracker
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Follow a trail ----'''Special: Prowess'''
Tracker is a [[:Category:Prowesses|Prowess]], this special kind of [[:Category:Feats|Feat]] is an action that can only be done once per scenario. To acquire it, you need to already have another Feat related to it, for example : [[Eye for Detail]], [[Photographic Memory]], [[Stealth]], [[Falconry]] or [[Survival]] Your Game Master may accept to waive such prerequisites if your background or in game actions justify it. ---- '''Description'''
The character is particularly good at following trails or tracking someone. He will automatically notice all hints that would help him find a criminal or prey that he is following. If it is humanely possible to follow the trail, he will succeed. For example it is not humanely possible to follow the trail of a person in a crowded building, unless the building is very dusty or if the person had mud on his shoes. The game master have to tell you if it is possible or not before using the feat. He will also know if he is being followed. [[Category:Official]] [[Category:Prowesses]] [[Category:Feats]]

{{Feats
| Synonyms = Follow a trail
| Prowess? = Yes
| Prowess Prerequisites = [[Eye for Detail]], [[Photographic Memory]], [[Stealth]], [[Falconry]] or [[Survival]]
| Official? = Yes
| Special = Leave blank if none.
| Description = 

The character is particularly good at following trails or tracking someone.

He will automatically notice all hints that would help him find a criminal or prey that he is following.
If it is humanely possible to follow the trail, he will succeed.

For example it is not humanely possible to follow the trail of a person in a crowded building, unless the building is very dusty or if the person had mud on his shoes.

The game master have to tell you if it is possible or not before using the feat.

He will also know if he is being followed.

}}

feats.title: Trickster
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Liar, Con Artist, Actor, Social Chameleon ---- '''Description'''
The character is able to build very convincing lies and play any role. It is extremely difficult not to believe the lies created by the character. He is able to create a coherent story and play any role. He cleverly anticipates all attempts to unmask him. He is able to take a role for years without being unmasked. He also has some skills of forgery of documents and disguise. He can read and write. In the limit of credibility and the appreciation of game master, everyone will believe the lies of the character. This Feat is difficult to detect and can not be detected with [[Detect Lies]], [[Instinct]] or [[Physiognomist]] This Feat is not a drawback. Thus the character is not necessarily a mythomaniac or con artist, but he has the ability to lie if the situation requires it. [[Category:Official]] [[Category:General Feats]] [[Category:Feats]]

}}

feats.title: True Strike
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Marksman, Lethal Poison, Swashbuckler, Botte Secrète ----'''Special: Prowess'''
True Strike is a [[:Category:Prowesses|Prowess]], this special kind of [[:Category:Feats|Feat]] is an action that can only be done once per scenario. To acquire it, you need to already have another Feat related to it, for example : [[Marksmanship]] (for throwing or ranged), [[Combat]] (for melee or unarmed), [[Strong]] (for melee or unarmed), [[Knowledge (apothecary)]] (for throwing or ranged) or [[Poison]] (for throwing or ranged) Your Game Master may accept to waive such prerequisites if your background or in game actions justify it. ---- '''Description'''
Using finesse, training, brute strength or potent poisons, you're capable of delivery deadly blows. When taking this Prowess, you have to choose a specialisation. It can be [[True Strike (unarmed)]], [[True Strike (melee)]], [[True Strike (throwing)]] or [[True Strike (ranged)]] When using this Prowess and to the extend of the humanely possible you will kill a normal person of your choice with your bare hands, with a melee weapon, using a throwing weapon or with a ranged weapon (bow, crossbow and the like) depending on your specialisation. Each types of weapons have a range limitation, for example it is not humanely possible to kill someone with a throwing dagger at more than 30 meters. You have to convince the Game Master that your weapon can be appropriately used with this Feat, for example a bread loaf cannot be considered an appropriate melee weapon, but a quarterstaff can. The Game Master may rule that an enemy is special and can't be killed by this Prowess, he has to warn you before using the Feat. On a 5 or a 6 on a die 6, this Feat can defeat an opponent that would require the [[Combat (monster)]] Feat. [[Category:Official]] [[Category:Prowesses]] [[Category:Feats]]

feats.title: Trumpets of Ruin
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Blight, Perdition, Devastation ----'''Special: Prowess'''
Trumpets of Ruin is a [[:Category:Prowesses|Prowess]], this special kind of [[:Category:Feats|Feat]] is an action that can only be done once per scenario. To acquire it, you need to already have another Feat related to it, for example : [[⛧ Dark Faith]] and character at least level 5 Your Game Master may accept to waive such prerequisites if your background or in game actions justify it. ----'''Special: Hidden'''
Trumpets of Ruin is a Hidden Feat. It can only be given to you by the Game Master and you cannot talk about this Feat with other Players. [[Category:Hidden]] ---- '''Description'''
To sound the Trumpets of Ruin is to summon not the justice of Heaven, but the laughter of Hell. Where the Apocalypse is scourge and judgment, the Trumpets are mockery and desecration — calamities loosed not for righteousness, but for cruelty. The visions of John spoke of angels who sounded their horns and heralded woe upon the world; but when the Trumpets of Ruin are called upon, it is the abyss itself that answers. Earth quakes, fire spreads, vermin swarm, and darkness covers the land — not as chastisement, but as ruin for its own sake. This Prowess emulates the effects of the [[Apocalypse]] Feat, but in a far more gruesome and merciless fashion. Its horrors fall chiefly upon the faithful and innocent, slaughtering pious Christians, while the corrupt, the wicked, and the damned are mostly spared. [[Category:Official]][[Category:Dark Feats]] [[Category:Prowesses]] [[Category:Feats]]

{{Feats

| Prowess? = Yes
| Prowess Prerequisites = [[⛧ Dark Faith]] and character at least level 5
| Hidden? = Yes
| Dark Feat? = Yes
| Official? = Yes
| Archetype? = No
| Description = 

To sound the Trumpets of Ruin is to summon not the justice of Heaven, but the laughter of Hell. Where the Apocalypse is scourge and judgment, the Trumpets are mockery and desecration — calamities loosed not for righteousness, but for cruelty.  

The visions of John spoke of angels who sounded their horns and heralded woe upon the world; but when the Trumpets of Ruin are called upon, it is the abyss itself that answers. Earth quakes, fire spreads, vermin swarm, and darkness covers the land — not as chastisement, but as ruin for its own sake.  

This Prowess emulates the effects of the [[Apocalypse]] Feat, but in a far more gruesome and merciless fashion. Its horrors fall chiefly upon the faithful and innocent, slaughtering pious Christians, while the corrupt, the wicked, and the damned are mostly spared.

}}

feats.title: Vanish
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Disappear ----'''Special: Prowess'''
Vanish is a [[:Category:Prowesses|Prowess]], this special kind of [[:Category:Feats|Feat]] is an action that can only be done once per scenario. To acquire it, you need to already have another Feat related to it, for example : [[Stealth]], [[Agile]], [[Instinct]] or [[Reflexes]] Your Game Master may accept to waive such prerequisites if your background or in game actions justify it. ---- '''Description'''
In the blink of an eye, the character is able to distract all of it's enemies and hide somewhere. He may or may not be able to move from his position without being seen, but will remain unseen for a certain time if he doesn't move. [[Category:Official]] [[Category:Prowesses]] [[Category:Feats]]

}}

Advices on creating Feats :

A Feat is a massive advantage granted to the finest of the finest after a life of training. Most normal people don't have Feats at all.
A person with the Agile Feat, is qualified to be in the Olympics (not win, but at least participate)
A person with the Rich Feat is the richest man in his city.
Etc..

And finally, make sure that the Feat is useful but not too useful, this depends on the scenarios and campaign you're playing.

A disadvantage that grants two feats freely chosen.

The goal of an Archetype is to guide a new player on how to create a very cool and coherent character.


Advices on creating Prowesses :

A Prowess is a type of non-visual spell. It is possible only if there is an explanation to the limit of the scientifically plausible and it is up to the player to justify it with the game master before using his prowess.

For example, it is humanly impossible to heal someone who has been decapitated, but it is not impossible to treat someone who's throat have been slit. It is very difficult, very rare, but scientifically plausible.

Unlike normal Feats, a character can acquire the same Prowess several times. 

-->

feats.title: Voice of the Abyss
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Words of Madness ----'''Special: Prowess'''
Voice of the Abyss is a [[:Category:Prowesses|Prowess]], this special kind of [[:Category:Feats|Feat]] is an action that can only be done once per scenario. To acquire it, you need to already have another Feat related to it, for example : Both [[Instill Madness]] and [[Puppet Master]] Your Game Master may accept to waive such prerequisites if your background or in game actions justify it. ----'''Special: Hidden'''
Voice of the Abyss is a Hidden Feat. It can only be given to you by the Game Master and you cannot talk about this Feat with other Players. [[Category:Hidden]] ---- '''Description'''
Through a potent combination of carefully chosen words, psychological manipulation, or a precisely administered poison, you unleash a devastating assault on your target's mind. By exploiting their deepest fears and vulnerabilities, you simultaneously break their sanity and implant a hypnotic program that controls their actions under specific conditions. The subject is under the effects of both [[Instill Madness]] and [[Puppet Master]] (instantaneously). The Game Master may rule that a person is special and can't be influenced by this Prowess, he has to warn you before using the Feat. In particular, this Feat can't be used on someone with the [[Courage]] feat. [[Category:Official]][[Category:Dark Feats]] [[Category:Prowesses]] [[Category:Feats]]

feats.title: Weak
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Venerable Age, Handicapped, Wounded, Leper ----'''Special: Hidden'''
Weak is a Hidden Feat. It can only be given to you by the Game Master and you cannot talk about this Feat with other Players. [[Category:Hidden]] ----'''Special'''
This Feat brings no advantage. It could be a permanent condition of your character or a temporary one. By default it is [[Weak (temporary)]] and will disappear at the next scenario. But if it is specified that it is permanent [[Weak (permanent)]] you would need a [[Miracle]] to cure it. [[Faith]] can always be used to prevent your character from receiving this condition. ---- '''Description'''
Your character is physically weak. This may come from a very bad injury, a disease, a poison, a handicap or simply old age. You will fail every time a complicated physical action is required. If you have some Feats associated to physical actions, you can use them only once per scenario, after that you need to roll a die 6 every time you use them. On a 5 or 6, you may use your Feat normally as if not wounded at all. The Game Master may decide that some physical Feats (like [[Stealth]], [[Lock Picking]] or [[Survival]]) are unaffected by this Feat. [[Category:Official]] [[Category:Feats]]

You will fail every time a complicated physical action is required. If you have some Feats associated to physical actions, you can use them only once per scenario, after that you need to roll a die 6 every time you use them. On a 5 or 6, you may use your Feat normally as if not wounded at all.

The Game Master may decide that some physical Feats (like [[Stealth]], [[Lock Picking]] or [[Survival]]) are unaffected by this Feat.

}}

feats.title: Well Connected
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Secret Society, Influential ---- '''Description'''
By birth or by will, the character has a very large network of high-ranking people who owe him favors. This does not make him a particularly rich person, but gives him a strong influence inside many organizations. He may obtain priviledges or favors for himself or another person. He is accompanied by an additional loyal henchman. He also may be of noble birth. [[Category:Official]] [[Category:General Feats]] [[Category:Feats]]

feats.title: Well Prepared
feats.content:
'''Synonyms:''' Hidden Object ----'''Special: Prowess'''
Well Prepared is a [[:Category:Prowesses|Prowess]], this special kind of [[:Category:Feats|Feat]] is an action that can only be done once per scenario. To acquire it, you need to already have another Feat related to it, for example : No prerequisites, anyone can take this Prowess. Your Game Master may accept to waive such prerequisites if your background or in game actions justify it. ---- '''Description'''
Thanks to constant paranoia and careful preparations, you happen to have the exact item you need right now ! It could be anything that you can carry and afford. A rope, a bow with arrows, a chain mail, a bar to block a door, a bottle of alcohol. Anything at all. The only limitation is that it should be something easy to get, for example this cannot be an antidote, a smoke bomb or a powerful poison. [[Category:Official]] [[Category:Prowesses]] [[Category:Feats]]
